# UC-10.7.716 · SAP NetWeaver Visual Composer Exploitation Attempt

## Description

This detection identifies SAP NetWeaver Visual Composer Exploitation Attempt. It supports security monitoring and incident response. Part of Splunk Security Essentials (ESCU) where applicable.

## Value

This detection identifies SAP NetWeaver Visual Composer Exploitation Attempt. It supports security monitoring and incident response. Part of Splunk Security Essentials (ESCU) where applicable.

## Implementation

ESCU Hunting detection — not designed for automated alerting. Run ad-hoc or on a low-frequency schedule from Splunk Enterprise Security for analyst-driven investigation. Requires Suricata ingested and CIM-normalized.

## Detailed Implementation

Enterprise Security Detection Rule

"SAP NetWeaver Visual Composer Exploitation Attempt" is a Hunting detection designed for analyst-driven threat hunting rather than automated alerting, sourced from the Splunk Enterprise Security Content Update (ESCU). Hunting detections are not intended for automated alerting. They are run on-demand or on a low-frequency schedule by security analysts investigating specific threat hypotheses.

Prerequisites

• Splunk Enterprise Security 7.x or later with the ES Content Update (ESCU) app installed and up to date.
• Data sources: Suricata. Must be ingested into Splunk and normalized to the Common Information Model (CIM) via the appropriate Technology Add-on.
• MITRE ATT&CK: T1190. Review the ATT&CK matrix for adjacent techniques to identify detection coverage gaps in your environment.

Deployment

1. In Enterprise Security, navigate to Configure → Content → Content Management.
2. Search for "SAP NetWeaver Visual Composer Exploitation Attempt" or filter by Analytic Story.
3. Hunting detections are typically left disabled for automated scheduling. Instead, run them on-demand from the Search bar or schedule at low frequency (daily or weekly).
4. Review results manually — hunting detections cast a wider net and expect analyst judgment to separate signal from noise.
5. When results warrant further investigation, create a Notable Event manually or initiate your incident response workflow.

Tuning and False Positive Management

Known false positives for this detection: Build-specific tuning depends on your environment. Review the alert with recent change, patch, and project context before global suppression — prefer scoped filters to preserve coverage.

• Hunting detections are expected to produce broader result sets that require analyst interpretation. Focus on refining the search scope (time range, specific hosts or users) rather than eliminating all noise.
• Maintain a hunting journal documenting hypotheses tested, results found, and detection improvements identified.
• Share high-fidelity findings with the detection engineering team to convert recurring hunting patterns into automated TTP or Anomaly detections.

Analyst Response Workflow

When reviewing Hunting results:

1. Run the search manually or review scheduled results. Evaluate each returned event against your threat hypothesis — not every result indicates compromise.
2. Cross-reference findings with current threat intelligence, recent security advisories, and outputs from other detection sources.
3. If suspicious activity is confirmed, create a Notable Event or escalate directly to your incident response process with the supporting evidence.
4. Document the hunting exercise: hypothesis, data sources queried, time range, findings, and any detection engineering improvements identified.

Validation

**Validate in production**

• Compare a sample of events with the source-of-truth console for the data source (cloud admin portal, device UI, or directory) covering the same time range.
• If you route findings through Enterprise Security, check the **Incident Review** queue and correlation search health so notables and drilldowns line up with what you see in search.


## SPL

```spl
| tstats count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime FROM datamodel=Web.Web
      WHERE (
            Web.url IN ("/CTCWebService/CTCWebServiceBean", "/VisualComposer/services/DesignTimeService", "/ctc/CTCWebService/CTCWebServiceBean")
        )
        AND Web.http_method IN ("HEAD", "POST") AND Web.status=200
      BY Web.src, Web.dest, Web.http_method,
         Web.url, Web.http_user_agent, Web.url_length,
         sourcetype
    | `drop_dm_object_name("Web")`
    | eval action=case(http_method="HEAD", "Recon/Probe", http_method="POST", "Possible Exploitation")
    | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
    | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
    | table firstTime, lastTime, src, dest, http_method, action, url, user_agent, url_length, sourcetype
    | `sap_netweaver_visual_composer_exploitation_attempt_filter`
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Web.Web by Web.src Web.url span=1h | sort -count
```

Table, Timeline (from ESCU).
